questions.question: What is the most effective way to handle the 'What if you get hit by a bus?' (Continuity) objection from a Legal department?

options.text: Tell them you are very healthy and exercise daily.
Ignore the question and focus on the product features.
Provide a 'Continuity Protocol' that details your technical architecture, data portability, and third-party maintenance partnerships.
Ask them not to be so morbid.

questions.explanation: Legal stakeholders care about risk mitigation, not your personal health. A technical continuity protocol demonstrates that the business asset (the software/service) is decoupled from your physical presence.

questions.question: Why should you avoid negotiating 'Redlines' exclusively over email?

options.text: Because email creates asynchronous friction and allows legal departments to stall; a direct call forces resolution and clarifies intent behind the changes.
Because you want to charge for the phone calls.
Because email is not legally binding.

questions.explanation: Asynchronous communication is the enemy of momentum. A 1:1 call with a legal stakeholder allows you to explain the 'Why' behind your terms, which often resolves their concerns much faster than a text-based back-and-forth.

questions.question: What is the 'Trust Bundle'?

options.text: A collection of proactive documents (Security overview, MSA, Insurance, Privacy policy) designed to pre-emptively satisfy the Shadow Committee.

questions.explanation: Speed is your advantage as a solo founder. By providing all the risk-mitigation data before Legal even asks for it, you look like a much larger and more prepared organization.

questions.question: Imagine a Procurement officer asks for a 15% discount at the literal last second of a deal. Write your response script that defends the price using the 'Scope vs. Price' or 'Alternative Terms' (Payment window/Length) strategy.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate if the student defends the value without being aggressive. The response should offer a non-price concession (like terms) or re-anchor to the ROI, rather than just giving in to the discount.
